concept.title: Key Resolution
concept.shortdesc: Use keys for indirect addressing and content reuse in your DITA projects. DitaCraft implements a fully spec-compliant DITA 1.3 key space construction algorithm including keyref chains, keyscope inheritance, and resolution diagnostics.

section.title: Overview
section.p: DITA keys provide a powerful mechanism for indirect addressing. Instead of using direct file paths, you define keys in your map and reference them throughout your topics. This enables:

ul.li: Easy updates when file locations change
Conditional content through key scopes
Centralized management of reusable content
Simplified localization workflows

section.title: Defining Keys
section.p: Keys are defined in DITA maps using <keydef> elements or the keys attribute on <topicref>.

section.title: Using keyref
section.p: Reference keys using the keyref attribute to pull in text content or create links.
section.codeblock:
Welcome to !

Welcome to DitaCraft!

section.title: Using conkeyref
section.p: The conkeyref attribute combines key resolution with content referencing. It first resolves the key to a file, then pulls content from a specific element.
This resolves shared-warnings to shared/warnings.dita, then pulls the element with id="safety-warning".

section.title: Key Space
section.p: DitaCraft builds a key space from your root map. The key space includes:

ul.li: All key definitions in the root map
Keys from referenced submaps
Keys inherited through key scopes

section.p: Key Precedence: When the same key is defined multiple times, the first definition wins. Keys defined earlier in the map take precedence.

section.title: Key Scopes
section.p: Key scopes allow you to define variations of the same content for different contexts.
section.codeblock:
<topicgroup keyscope="admin"> <keydef keys="audience"> <topicmeta><keywords><keyword>administrators</keyword></keywords></topicmeta> </keydef> <topicref href="admin-guide.dita"/> </topicgroup>
<topicgroup keyscope="user"> <keydef keys="audience"> <topicmeta><keywords><keyword>end users</keyword></keywords></topicmeta> </keydef> <topicref href="user-guide.dita"/> </topicgroup>

section.title: Keyref Chains
section.p: A keyref chain occurs when a key definition itself uses a keyref attribute to point to another key, forming a multi-hop chain. DitaCraft follows the full chain to resolve the final target.
section.codeblock:
<!-- root.ditamap --> <keydef keys="product" href="topics/product.dita"/>
<!-- submap.ditamap with @keyscope="enterprise" --> <keydef keys="product" keyref="product"/>
section.p: When resolving keyref="enterprise.product", DitaCraft follows the chain through the scoped alias back to the root definition in product.dita.
section.note: Circular keyref chains are detected and short-circuited to prevent infinite loops.

section.title: Keyscope Inheritance
section.p: DitaCraft implements the DITA 1.3 PushDown inheritance algorithm for key scopes. When a map element has a @keyscope attribute, its keys are made available under scope-qualified names throughout the parent map.
All keys in submap.ditamap are accessible as product-a.keyname from the parent map.

p.b: Scope on a non-map topicref (inline branch):

section.p: DitaCraft treats @keyscope on a non-map topicref as an inline scope branch: keys defined within that branch are qualified under draft.* while still being accessible by their short names within the branch itself.
Multiple scope names: A single element can declare multiple scopes separated by spaces (keyscope="a b"). DitaCraft creates qualified aliases under each scope name.

section.title: Scope Explosion Protection
section.p: Deeply nested key scopes can produce a very large number of qualified key aliases (one per scope combination). DitaCraft enforces a cap of 50,000 key space entries to prevent excessive memory use and slow builds.
When the cap is reached:

ul.li: DitaCraft stops generating additional scope aliases
A scopeExplosionWarning flag is set on the key space
All keys defined before the cap was reached remain available

section.p: In practice, this limit is only reached with extremely large maps that have many deeply nested key scopes. Normal DITA projects are not affected.

section.title: Key Provenance
section.p: Every key definition in DitaCraft's key space records its source line number (1-based) within the map file where it was defined. This information is used by:

ul.li: Go to Definition — jumps to the exact line of the <keydef> element
The Key Space View — shows the source location in tooltips
Hover on keyref — displays the definition location alongside the resolved target

section.p: For scope-qualified aliases generated from a nested scope, the source line is inherited from the original key definition.

section.title: Key Resolution Diagnostics
section.p: DitaCraft provides tools for understanding exactly how a key was resolved, which is useful for debugging complex map hierarchies.
When you hover over a keyref or conkeyref attribute, the hover tooltip shows:

ul.li: The resolved target file or inline content
The key scope context used for resolution
The source map and line where the key was defined
Any keyref chain steps that were followed

section.p: The underlying explainKey() API (used by the LSP server) returns a full KeyResolutionReport containing every lookup step, the effective key space, and the complete keyref chain — useful for extension authors building on top of DitaCraft.

section.title: DitaCraft Key Support Summary
section.p: DitaCraft provides full DITA 1.3 spec-compliant key support:

ul.li: Key Space Construction: BFS traversal with full cascade algorithm, peer map handling, and fallback key definitions
Keyref Chains: Multi-hop resolution with correct scope prefix tracking
Keyscope Inheritance: PushDown algorithm for map-level scopes and inline branch scopes on non-map topicrefs
Scope Explosion Protection: 50,000-entry cap with warning flag
Provenance Tracking: Source line recorded for every key definition
Navigation: Ctrl+Click on keyref and conkeyref navigates to the key's target with Go to Definition across files
Caching: Key space cached with 5-minute TTL, automatic invalidation on map changes
Root Map Selection: Explicitly set the root map via DITA: Set Root Map
Validation: Undefined and missing keys flagged as diagnostics
